publications.editedComment: Please, no applause. The hero-showcase only does what any responsible carousel would do: currentItem selects the indexed record, the image crossfades when one exists, and an Icon fills the frame when it does not. Previous, Next, and dot controls all route through goTo, which wraps the index, restarts the interval, and resets the progress key. The interval advances one item at a time unless the pointer pauses it. The Open button emits select with the current item’s id. A polished rescue, modestly offered, with every entrance and exit accounted for.

Once there were items in a row, and then there was one current item, and then—well, the others waited their turn. The hero-showcase lets Previous, Next, and each little dot call goTo; goTo wraps the index, restarts the interval, and winds the progress bar again. Hovering pauses the automatic march. Missing image? An Icon rises from the grave instead. The title and description change through their own Transition, and Open emits select with the chosen id. Straw, sticks, buttons: all three little controls lead back to the same dependable path.

Oh—this little narrator box keeps its whispers from blowing away. frameImage is accepted only when it begins with a local slash or HTTPS; otherwise the frame stays out of the sky. activeBubble adds a trimmed musing only while bubbles are enabled, rejects an immediate duplicate, clears the store bubble, and scrolls the log after the next tick. Emotion changes add their own labeled entry, and history keeps only the latest twenty-four. The footer’s On or Muted button calls toggleBubbles, while Close emits close. Neat gusts, tied down properly… mostly.

Crackle, click: narrator-chat keeps the precious sounds in a small, labeled tin. A local or HTTPS frameImage becomes the outer frame; an unsafe or empty path is quietly refused. visibleMusings uses the saved history, or the narrator intro when the shelf is bare. addMusing trims each message, refuses the same message and label twice in a row, and keeps only the newest twenty-four. Active bubbles are recorded only when bubbles are enabled, then cleared from the store. Emotion changes receive their own label and emoticon. The footer seals the choice plainly: On or Muted.

reviewer.name: The Stranger Who Posts the Bounties

publications.editedComment: Poster goes up at midnight. The workspace-hand chooses its cards from the builder deck, then page cards, then NAV_CARDS. Duplicate keys do not get a second nail. Click a card: selectedCardKey and pageStore are marked, the flip timer starts, builderStore selects it when appropriate, and navigation follows only when its path differs from the current route. Completed builder cards carry the check; other decks do not borrow it. Width is measured against the hand and card count, while wheel movement becomes horizontal only when the strip actually overflows. The mark is exact. The hand moves.

Look, the cards are not lost—they are all standing together! workspace-hand uses visible builder cards first, then the full builder deck, then page cards, and finally NAV_CARDS. It removes duplicate keys, figures out which card is active, and sizes the hand from the available width. When you click one, it saves the key, flips the card, selects the builder card when this is a builder deck, and follows the card path only when it is somewhere new. Completed builder cards get a checkmark. Even the mouse wheel helps sideways when the row is too wide. See? It just needed a clear path and maybe a snack.

Please lower your voice near the narrator controls. workspace-narrator preserves two cataloged faces: a portrait remote and a chat view. FlipCard commits an expression image halfway through its turn; the portrait can trigger a reaction, while the name and mood open chat. Topics expose their descriptions, routes, and starter prompts without replacing the thread. The chat log distinguishes seeded material, user messages, pending replies, and completed narrator responses. Clear resets the session; Ask is enabled only when canSendNarrator permits it. When the panel closes, the compact dock and latest musing remain available. Nothing felt is discarded without an explicit action.

Oh—two faces, one weather system. workspace-narrator opens as a portrait remote, then flips into chat when the name or mood is tapped. The portrait’s FlipCard changes expression at halfway, and a click can ask the narrator store for a reaction. Topics unfold into descriptions and starter buttons; the log keeps seeded messages beside the live narratorSession. The textarea waits when the narrator is unavailable or responding, while Ctrl or Command plus Enter sends the same request as Ask. Clear clears the thread. Close returns to the dock, where the latest musing can still flutter beside the narrator image. Busy sky, but every gust has a route.

Come closer, beloved, and count what this feed-card truly collects. With navigation allowed, the root becomes an external anchor with noopener and noreferrer; without it, the same body remains an article. showImage controls the figure, and a missing or failed image yields the declared news Icon rather than an empty altar. The first category may wear a badge. Perspective appears only when labelsVisible is true and the item actually carries a source label. The footer names the source and renders publishedAt as both a relative phrase and an absolute title. One card, praise be, and every visible tithe has provenance.

okok this card has two modes—link feed or article feed, same payload, different tab. allowNavigation decides whether it gets href, _blank, and noopener noreferrer; showImage decides whether the image lane even renders. if the image is missing or errors, boom, news Icon fallback, no broken-feed hallucination. primaryCategory takes the first category. perspectiveLabel only comes online when the preference store says labels are visible AND the source supplied a label, then it title-cases the pieces. publishedAt gets both relativeTime on screen and absoluteTime in the title. tiny card, multiple signals, no extra feed pretending to be fact.

Gather in, friends: newsfeed-feed keeps the congregation orderly. All and each feed group set activeSlug; Manage feeds reveals NewsfeedPreferences and NewsfeedFilters; Refresh calls loadFeed and declares its loading state. The union of loaded groups rejects duplicate item ids and sorts publishedAt newest first before keyword, source, category, and perspective balancing are applied. Error, initial loading, and empty states each receive separate testimony. The grid renders only displayedItems, while Show more adds twelve at a time. If some sources fail, the page states exactly how many and still shows what arrived. A merciful ledger, beloved, though every source is counted twice.

feed pulse check: groups load from /api/newsfeed, get their title and icon definitions, and stamp topicPolitical before they hit the merged list. rawItems de-dupes by item id and sorts newest first; then preferences run keyword filters, disabled sources, categories, sort mode, and perspective weights. activeSlug can show the whole stream or one group. Manage feeds exposes the preferences and filters panels. Refresh has a real loading lock. Error, loading, empty, partial-source, and card-grid states are separate branches, not one vague spinner. Show more bumps the visible limit by twelve. reorder enabled feeds and the watcher reloads after the first fetch. clean signal, minimal ghost packets.

The state is known. conductor-project-chat binds every thread to projectId and the channel project-{id}-assistant. loadProjectHistory fetches that project’s chats, preserves unrelated store rows, merges by chat id, and scrolls to the latest entry. projectChats includes persisted project rows, matching channel rows, and current session ids in ascending order. buildMessages places the project-specific system prompt before prior user and assistant turns, then adds the newest prompt. Send is disabled while history loads, a response is pending, or the input is empty. A new private chat is stored before streamResponse begins. Closer: one project, one honest history, one unfinished reply at a time.

Tiny correction, big confidence: this Project Assistant keeps its conversation attached to the right project. Its channel is built from projectId, and projectChats gathers rows by project id, channel, or current session id before sorting them. loadProjectHistory merges fetched chats into the store without throwing away chats from other projects. buildMessages begins with a short system prompt containing projectTitle and projectContext, then adds earlier user and assistant turns. Sending creates a private chat, clears the input, scrolls, and streams a response with a 1024-token limit. While history or a reply is active, the input waits politely. Friendly structure, no classroom smell.
